name: Dinosaurs: What If Every Dinosaur From Every Period Combined Together?!
description:
Summary of a Combined Mesozoic Period (Triassic, Jurassic, and Cretaceous Simultaneous)

Imagine a world where the beginnings, the peak, and the end of the Age of Dinosaurs all existed at the very same time:

1. The Land and Continents

  • Pangea's Split: The world would be a chaotic mix of the supercontinent Pangea (characteristic of the Triassic) still intact, alongside the emerging, fragmented continents of the Jurassic and Cretaceous periods (Laurasia and Gondwana, breaking into smaller modern-day landmasses). This would lead to enormous geographic diversity, from vast, dry central deserts to new, lush coastal environments.

  • Volcanic Activity: Extreme volcanic activity would be happening globally, as the rifting and breaking of Pangea—which occurred over the entire era—would be accelerated into a single, massive event.

  • Sea Levels: Shallow inland seas, like the massive Western Interior Seaway that cut North America in half during the Cretaceous, would exist simultaneously with the earliest, smaller oceans of the Triassic, resulting in a planet with complex and rapidly shifting coastlines.

2. The Dinosaur Life

  • Ultimate Diversity: Every major group of non-avian dinosaur would live side-by-side.

    • Early Dinosaurs like Herrerasaurus (Triassic) would compete for resources with the largest Sauropods like Argentinosaurus (Cretaceous).

    • Iconic Predators like Allosaurus (Jurassic) would hunt alongside the massive Tyrannosaurus rex (Cretaceous).

    • Armor and Horns from the Jurassic (Stegosaurus) and the Cretaceous (Triceratops and Ankylosaurus) would all co-exist.

  • The Skies and Seas:

    • Pterosaurs would range from the small, early types to the gigantic Quetzalcoatlus with its 30 ft wingspan.

    • The oceans would be filled with all the great marine reptiles: Ichthyosaurs, Plesiosaurs, and Mosasaurs competing fiercely for food.

  • Flora and Fauna: The environment would be a botanical mashup:

    • Giant conifers, ferns, and cycads (characteristic of the Jurassic) would grow next to the earliest flowering plants (Angiosperms) that emerged in the Cretaceous.

    • The first small true mammals (Triassic/Jurassic) would scamper beneath the feet of all the immense dinosaurs.

3. The Enduring Challenge

  • Continuous Extinction Stress: This "combined" world would not last long. It would be a moment of maximum biodiversity that immediately leads to a catastrophic end. The final moments would involve the asteroid impact that ended the Cretaceous Period.

In essence, a combined Mesozoic Period would represent an unimaginably crowded and ecologically volatile world, where all 186 million years of evolution, continental drift, and environmental change are compressed into a single, spectacular, and ultimately doomed epoch.
creator: jasmine_the_witch_hersheys
